llcFilesWith: West Virginia Secretary of State, Business & Licensing Division (trade name/DBA registration, Form TN-1, filed through the One Stop Business Portal); the business must first be registered with the West Virginia State Tax Department
soleProprietorFilesWith: West Virginia Secretary of State (the same trade name registration; individuals and sole proprietors register with the State Tax Department and then the Secretary of State)
feeBasis: The Secretary of State fee to register a trade name is $25, plus a $1 online processing fee for online filings.
renewalRule: A trade name registration stays in effect until the owner files a certificate of withdrawal when it stops using the name (§ 47-8-4); there is no periodic renewal.
protectionNote: The Secretary of State states plainly that trade names are not protected from use by other businesses through registration; a business that wants exclusive rights files a trademark or service mark instead.
searchNote: Registered trade names appear in the Secretary of State's business entity search.
notes: West Virginia registers trade names centrally with the Secretary of State (W. Va. Code ch. 47, art. 8). LLCs and sole proprietors alike file a trade name (DBA) registration for $25 after the business is registered with the State Tax Department; the fastest path is the One Stop Business Portal. A registration does not expire and is ended by filing a certificate of withdrawal. Registration is notice only: the Secretary of State says trade names are not protected from use by other businesses.
